full_text:
Tribus project update

07.12.2023

Overview of Tribus

Update since previous meeting

Faster: using minisom for unsupervised clustering
~5min to annotate 600,000 cells
Add hyperparameter tuning for unsupervised clustering
Conclude data transformation method
The (preprocessed) datasets should be normalized via an arcsinh transformation with a co-factor of 5 (optional) and winsorized to [0%, 99.9%].
Benchmarking with other similar cell type calling methods
Expand the choosing of benchmarking datasets
Compare with: 
Prior knowledge based methods: ACDC, Astir
Unsupervised clustering method: Phenograph
Write the manuscript

5 Datasets are currently used to test Tribus

Name | # Cell & cell type | Introduction | Panel
AML | - N = 104184 cells
- 14 manually
gated cell classes | Human bone marrow from both Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) patients and healthy adult donors | Mass cytometry with a panel of 32 markers
BMMC | - N = 61725 cells
- 24 manually gated cell populations | Human bone marrow mononuclear cells | Mass cytometry with 13 markers
DCIS | - 69672 cells
- 23 cell types | Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS). 79 clinically annotated surgical resections, including normal breast tissue (N = 9, reduction mammoplasty), primary DCIS (N = 58), and IBC (N = 12) | MIBI-TOF with a 37-plex antibody staining panel
HubMAP | - 25 cell types
- 137652 + 110635 | 8 regions from the human small bowel and the large intestine (4 each) | CODEX with 54 antibodies
Pre & Post | - No labels | 5 pairs of high-grade ovarian cancer samples collected before and after neoadjuvant chemotherapy (NACT) | whole-slide images generated by CyCIF

Tribus recovers fine-grained cell types as accurately as human experts

Heatmaps showing the median marker intensity of manually gated cell populations and Tribus classification from the DCIS dataset are similar.

Confusion Matrix

Tribus can target ambiguous populations and discover phenotypically new clusters

Supervised UMAPs representation of manual and Tribus labels on the DCIS dataset. 
Cell types were color-coded based on original and Tribus annotation, the same cell type label was assigned the same color.

Tribus outperformed than other similar methods in various scenarios

Adding levels in logic table helps with improving accuracy

The Napari plugin

Overview

Tiff

Image file format
Tiled images

Chunked, compressed, N-dimensional arrays
Also allows cloud computing
Divides large arrays into evenly spaced chunks
Only required chunks are read into memory
Each data chunk has a unique key 
For pyramid images you can load each resolution level as a separate dask array

Zarr + Dask

Visualizing assigned celltypes

Visualizing marker expression

Visualizing celltype probability

Tribus-Napari widget

Pre & Post Dataset

Overview

6 pairs of HGSOC samples, before and after neoadjuvant chemotherapy
No previous labels
Pre-samples: biopsies
Post-samples: whole slide images (CyCIF)
1 pair discarded (sample 8&9) during QC
Sample 12 discarded for insufficient staining
First design: separate logic table for each sample due to marker quality

GOAL: One unified logic table to analyze a subset of the samples

Sample overview

Samples with most working antibodies

Sample number in pairs | Segmented cells | True cells
01 | 15893 | 13473
10 | 743058 | 592442
02 | 14661 | 7899
04 | 992213 | 766229
03 | 25905 | 13468
05 | 5144696 | 443464
11 | 23066 | 19789
07 | 536400 | 439303
06 | 594224 | 519150

Logic table design

Global

Myeloid

Tumor

General markers with poor quality staining make building a uniform table difficult

Myeloids and stromal cells tend to get confused

Marker | Tumor | CD 8 | Myeloid | Stromal
CD3d | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0
E-cadherin | 1 | -1 | -1 | 0
Vimentin | -1 | 0 | 0 | 1
CK7 | 1 | 0 | -1 | -1
IBA1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0
CD8a | 0 | 1 | -1 | -1
CD163 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0

Marker | M2 | M1
CD168 | 1 | -1
IBA1 | 1 | 1

Marker | Proliferatin | Non-proliferating
Ki67 | 1 | -1
E-cadherin | 1 | 1
CK7 | 1 | 1

Examples

CD4 staining in Sample 1

CD4 and CD8 overlap in Sample 6

IBA1 and Vimentin in Sample1

General problem: 
Vimentin is strong and seen everywhere

Notes for Tribus users

If you only have Vimentin as a marker for stromal cells, it will likely make Tribus fail
If the other cell types clear enough, they might create a good enough negative constraint 
Merge myeloid and stromal cells, they might separate better on the second level
If not all subtype specific markers are working, try to look for a generic marker for the global level 
In dense areas even human eye can’t always distinguish cells on a single cell level
If you only have a few working markers, try creating an artificial ‘Other’ celltype category

Help me review: Is this the correct way to describe Tribus scoring function?
